title: Pre-monsoon showers across Karnataka; hailstorm in Bengaluru - Deccan Herald
article_text:
File photo for representational puropse.
Bengaluru: Many parts of Karnataka received first spell of pre-monsoon rainfall on Tuesday, bringing down summer heat.
In Bengaluru, heavy rainfall was accompanied by lightning and gusty wind, later topped with hailstorm, in Sanjayanagar, Yelahanka and Devanahalli.
There was moderate to heavy rainfall in Konanakunte, Kanakapura Road, RR Nagar, Banashankari 6th Stage, Sampigehalli, Kathriguppe, Horamavu and parts of CBD.
N Puviarasan, head of meteorological centre at India Meteorological Department, Bengaluru, confirmed hailstorm occurred for a short duration.
The rain had an immediate effect on maximum temperature, which fell by 1.3°C to 32.3°C in Bengaluru city and by 1.2°C to 32°C at HAL airport.
GBA's disaster management team has asked people to contact helpline 1533 or Whatsapp 94806 85700.
For Mar 18, IMD has forecast light to moderate rain/thundershowers at one or two places over Bengaluru Urban, Bengaluru Rural, Ramanagara, Tumakuru, Mandya, Chikkamagaluru, Shivamogga, Hassan, Kodagu, Davangere, Mysuru and Chamarajanagar.

title: Heavy Rain Alert: Heavy rains expected on March 18, 19, 20 and 21, Meteorological Department issues alert for these states - informalnewz
article_text:

Heavy Rain Alert: Heavy rains expected on March 18, 19, 20 and 21, Meteorological Department issues alert for these states
By
Shyamu Maurya
March 18, 2026
0
9
Share
Heavy Rain Alert Heavy rains expected on March 18, 19, 20 and 21, Meteorological Department issues alert for these states

Heavy Rain Alert: The pre-monsoon season is about to intensify across the country. The Meteorological Department has issued an alert for heavy rainfall in several states on March 18, 19, 20, and 21.

Add informalnewz.com as a Preferred Source







Heavy Rain Alert: The weather in the country has begun to change since the arrival of the pre-monsoon season. 2025 was a good year for the monsoon, and this season saw excellent rainfall. Many states saw more rainfall than ever before. The 2025 monsoon season was so excellent that even after this, the trend of rain continues in many states in 2026. Now, the pre-monsoon season will intensify in the country. Consequently, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a Heavy Rain Alert for several states on March 18, 19, 20, and 21.

Northwest India

The pre-monsoon season will intensify in northwest India. Consequently, the Meteorological Department has issued an alert that on March 18, 19, 20, and 21, there will be heavy rain in some areas, while light rain will occur in others in Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Haryana, Jammu and Kashmir, and Ladakh. During this period, there is also an alert of strong winds blowing at a speed of 40-50 kilometers per hour.

Eastern and Central India

Pre-monsoon will also intensify in eastern and central India. The Meteorological Department has issued a heavy rainfall alert for Madhya Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, Odisha, West Bengal, Sikkim, Chhattisgarh, and Vidarbha on March 18, 19, 20, and 21. During this period, strong winds with speeds of up to 50-70 kilometers per hour, lightning, and hailstorms are also expected in some districts.

Western India

The pre-monsoon will intensify in western India. Consequently, the Meteorological Department has issued an alert that Central Maharashtra and Marathwada will experience heavy rainfall on March 18, 19, 20, and 21. There is also an alert for thunderstorms and thunderstorms during this period.

South India

The pre-monsoon will intensify in southern India as well. Consequently, the Meteorological Department has issued an alert for heavy rainfall in Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, coastal Andhra Pradesh, Mahe, Puducherry, Yanam, and Karaikal on March 18, 19, 20, and 21. There is also an alert for strong winds in many areas during this period.

Northeast India

The pre-monsoon will intensify in northeastern India as well. Consequently, the Meteorological Department has issued an alert that heavy rainfall will occur in Meghalaya, Manipur, Assam, Mizoram, Tripura, and Nagaland on March 18, 19, 20, and 21. There is also an alert of lightning during this period.
